```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id
{
  if x.width >= PAD then x
  else Id(x.kind, x.value, PAD)
}

lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).kind == x.kind
  ensures Canonicalize(x).value == x.value
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width == if x.width >= PAD then x.width else PAD
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= PAD
  ensures Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))
  // idempotence: canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op
  ensures Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x)
{ }
```

**Explanation of the spec clauses:**

- `kind` and `value` are preserved exactly (the entity identity never changes).
- The resulting `width` is `max(x.width, PAD)`: ids already at ≥4 digits are returned unchanged, otherwise padded to exactly 4. This pins down the width completely.
- `width >= PAD` is the minimum-width guarantee (implied by the previous clause, but stated explicitly for clarity).
- `Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))` ensures the output width can still hold the value (canonicalization never produces an ill-formed id).
- The idempotence clause captures "canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op."
